projects.title: Making Financial Indexes Visible
projects.summary: A product interface that turned index-building from emails, spreadsheets, and scripts into a visual workflow.
projects.overview: Mapped the index-building process, shaped a modular interaction model, and designed screens that helped users build, validate, and launch strategies in days instead of weeks.

contentBlocks.body: S&P Dow Jones Indexes needed a clearer way to turn a complex, rule-based financial process into a product. The work focused on making the logic visible without making the interface feel heavy.

contentBlocks.alt: Case-study slide explaining the challenge behind the S&P index builder platform.
contentBlocks.caption: Project framing for a financial-index builder.

contentBlocks.alt: Case-study slide describing role, collaborators, and lessons from the index builder project.
contentBlocks.caption: Role, design rationale, and lessons from translating financial logic into UI.

contentBlocks.alt: Product interface screens for building and validating financial index strategies.
contentBlocks.caption: Interface states for building and validating index strategies.

contentBlocks.alt: Index builder landing screen with options to build, validate, and deploy an index.
contentBlocks.caption: Landing screen for building, validating, and deploying a strategic index.

takeaways: Make abstract rules visible before asking users to act.
A strong model helps technical and non-technical users work from the same idea.
Visual design can reduce ambiguity in complex financial tools.

projects.title: Clearer Flight Booking
projects.summary: An early travel UX project that made a multilingual booking journey clearer across search, selection, and upsell moments.
projects.overview: Mapped booking paths, audited existing interactions, restructured decision points, and built a custom design system and icon set for a large transactional airline site.

contentBlocks.body: This stays in the portfolio as an archive project. The visual language belongs to an earlier web era, but the work is still useful because it shows large-scale transactional UX, multilingual constraints, and design-system thinking.

contentBlocks.alt: Case-study slide describing UX and web design responsibilities for SunExpress.
contentBlocks.caption: Role and lessons from a large-scale airline booking project.

contentBlocks.alt: SunExpress website screens showing homepage, destination, and booking interface explorations.
contentBlocks.caption: Booking and destination-page explorations for the SunExpress website.

contentBlocks.alt: SunExpress booking screens for outbound and inbound flight choices.
contentBlocks.caption: Transactional screens across outbound and inbound flight choices.

contentBlocks.alt: Project notes about SunExpress accessibility, language, and scalable interaction patterns.
contentBlocks.caption: Project notes on accessibility, language, and scalable interaction patterns.

takeaways: Structure affects confidence in transactional flows.
Small content and ordering changes can reduce confusion without a full rebuild.
A design system is useful only when tested against real booking cases.

projects.title: Designing Trust Flows
projects.summary: Product and interaction work for identity and age-verification moments where users needed to understand what was happening and why.
projects.overview: Designed and documented verification flows across mobile and desktop, including QR handoffs, ID details, and face-scan explanations for sensitive user journeys.

contentBlocks.body: Identity products need to explain themselves at the moment of action. The work focused on trust, recovery, and clear handoffs between devices and services.

contentBlocks.alt: Yoti QR-code modal implementation flow across desktop and mobile.
contentBlocks.caption: QR-code handoff flow between desktop, mobile, and callback states.

contentBlocks.alt: Yoti mobile screen explaining why a face scan is required.
contentBlocks.caption: A sensitive verification moment explained in plain language.

contentBlocks.alt: Mobile ID and age-verification screens from Yoti.
contentBlocks.caption: Mobile identity states for age and document verification.

contentBlocks.alt: Yoti brand and logo documentation page.
contentBlocks.caption: Brand and interface documentation for consistent implementation.

takeaways: Trust depends on explaining the reason behind a request.
Cross-device flows need clear entry, waiting, and recovery states.
Sensitive products benefit from simple language and visible system feedback.

projects.title: Digital Calm for Luxury Ownership
projects.summary: Digital experience work that translated Bentley's brand tone into calm interaction flows, motion, and ownership moments.
projects.overview: Worked across concept, interaction design, storytelling, 3D, and motion. Built a digital garage in Blender, shaped interactive flows, and co-developed a visual and motion language for Bentley's digital presence.

contentBlocks.body: The work explored how a luxury digital product can feel calm, composed, and useful without losing the emotional quality of the brand.

contentBlocks.alt: Case-study slide describing role and lasting insight from Bentley digital work.
contentBlocks.caption: Role, process, and lessons from shaping Bentley's digital presence.

contentBlocks.alt: Bentley car on a road at sunset.
contentBlocks.caption: Brand atmosphere used to guide pacing, tone, and interaction decisions.

contentBlocks.alt: Bentley app and ownership screens arranged in a product layout.
contentBlocks.caption: Ownership and service touchpoints arranged as a digital product system.

contentBlocks.alt: Bentley digital strategy prompts and experience principles.
contentBlocks.caption: Strategy prompts for a connected Bentley ownership experience.

takeaways: Brand tone becomes stronger when it affects interaction decisions.
Pacing is a design material in premium digital products.
Prototypes help stakeholders discuss feeling, not only screens.

projects.displayTitle: A portfolio built as an environment
projects.title: Personal Experimental Website
projects.summary: A self-initiated website exploring how a portfolio can feel like an environment rather than a sequence of pages.
projects.overview: Designed and built a responsive web environment with spatial depth, dynamic interaction, motion, sound, and layered content. The project became a live system for testing browser performance, animation, and immersive navigation.

contentBlocks.body: This project documents the website as a design object: a personal system for testing how a portfolio can behave more like a space than a static set of pages.

contentBlocks.alt: Case-study slide describing the personal experimental website and its spatial design goals.
contentBlocks.caption: Project framing for a personal website built as a spatial environment.

contentBlocks.alt: Annotated website interface showing interaction and animation details.
contentBlocks.caption: Annotated interaction details for navigation, overlays, and motion states.

contentBlocks.alt: Website design board about creating smart user experiences.
contentBlocks.caption: Experience framing and visual direction for the site.

contentBlocks.alt: Dark immersive website screen with interface and depth effects.
contentBlocks.caption: Interface study for depth, atmosphere, and movement.

takeaways: A portfolio can teach through movement and structure, not only written case studies.
Immersion needs performance discipline to stay usable.
Personal work is strongest when it exposes how you think and build.

projects.title: Touch Typing Refined
projects.summary: A focused product concept for making typing practice feel clearer, calmer, and easier to return to.
projects.overview: Explored a learning product around touch typing, using simple feedback, mobile-friendly screens, and practice loops that support steady improvement instead of pressure.

contentBlocks.body: This stays in the set for now as a smaller product concept. It adds a different kind of work: focused learning UX rather than enterprise, travel, or identity systems.

contentBlocks.alt: Research project slide for an experimental portfolio and spatial web environment.
contentBlocks.caption: Early research framing for exploratory product and interface studies.

contentBlocks.alt: Touch Typing Refined product screens for a Pro Keyboard concept.
contentBlocks.caption: Mobile product screens for a touch-typing learning concept.

contentBlocks.alt: Minimal prototype screen with the Beck mark.
contentBlocks.caption: Minimal interface study for the wider concept system.

takeaways: Small concepts still need a clear user problem.
Learning tools should reward progress without adding pressure.
This project needs sharper evidence if it remains in the final selection.
